fields.name: TF1
fields.description: Timer1 overflow. Set to logic 1 by hardware on a Timer1 overflow. The flag is set to logic 0 by the CPU after 2 machine cycles.

fields.name: TR1
fields.description: Timer1 run control. Set by firmware only. When set to logic 1, Timer1 is enabled.

fields.name: TF0
fields.description: Timer0 overflow. Set by hardware on a Timer0 overflow. The flag is set to logic 0 by the CPU after 2 machine cycles.

fields.name: TR0
fields.description: Timer0 run control. Set by firmware only. When set to logic 1, Timer0 is enabled.

fields.name: IE1
fields.description: External Interrupt1 event. Set to logic 1 by hardware when an external interrupt is detected on P33_INT1.

fields.name: IT1
fields.description: External Interrupt1 control. Set by firmware only. When set to logic 1, Interrupt1 triggers on a falling edge of P33_INT1.

fields.name: IE0
fields.description: External Interrupt0 event. Set to logic 1 by hardware when an external interrupt is detected on P32_INT0.

fields.name: IT0
fields.description: External Interrupt0 control. Set by firmware only. When set to logic 1, Interrupt0 triggered by a falling edge on P32_INT0.

fields.name: GATE1
fields.description: Timer1 gate control. Set by firmware only. When set to logic 1, Timer1 is enabled only when P33_INT1 is high and bit TR1 of register T01CON is set.

fields.name: C/T1
fields.description: Timer1 timer/counter selector. Set by firmware only. When set to logic 1, Timer1 is set to counter operation.

fields.name: M11
fields.description: Timer1 mode bits M[11:10]

fields.name: M10
fields.description: Timer1 mode bits M[11:10]

fields.name: GATE0
fields.description: Timer0 gate control

fields.name: C/T0
fields.description: Timer0 timer/counter selector

fields.name: M01
fields.description: Timer0 mode bits M[01:00]

fields.name: M00
fields.description: Timer0 mode bits M[01:00]

fields.name: T0L
fields.description: Timer0 lower byte data

fields.name: T1L
fields.description: Timer1 lower byte data

fields.name: T0H
fields.description: Timer0 upper byte data

fields.name: T1H
fields.description: Timer1 upper byte data

fields.name: TF2
fields.description: Timer2 overflow Set to logic 1 by a Timer2 overflow. Set to logic 0 by firmware. TF2 is not set when in baud rate generation mode. The bit IE0_5 of register IE0 (see Table 11 on page 17) has to be set to logic 1 to enable the corresponding CPU interrupt.

fields.name: RCLK0
fields.description: Timer2 Debug UART Receive Clock selector. Set by firmware only. When set to logic 1, Debug UART uses Timer2 overflow pulses. When set to logic 0, Debug UART uses overflow pulses from another source (e.g. Timer1 in a standard configuration).

fields.name: TCLK0
fields.description: Timer2 Debug UART Transmit Clock selector. Set by firmware only. When set to logic 1, Debug UART uses Timer2 overflow pulses. When set to logic 0, Debug UART uses overflow pulses from another source (e.g. Timer1 in a standard configuration).

fields.name: TR2
fields.description: Timer2 Run control. Set by firmware only. When set to logic 1, Timer2 is started. When set to logic 0, Timer2 is stopped.

fields.name: C_T2
fields.description: Timer2 Counter/Timer selector. Set by firmware only. When set to logic 1, Timer2 is set to counter operation. When set to logic0, Timer2 is set to timer operation.

fields.name: RSVD_0
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: RSVD_3
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: RSVD_6
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: T2RD
fields.description: Timer2 ReaD flag. Set by hardware and firmware. This bit is set to logic 1 by hardware, if T2H is incremented between reading T2L and reading T2H. This bit is set to logic 0, on the trailing edge of next T2L read. This bit is used to indicate that the16 bit Timer2 register is not read properly since the T2H part was incremented by hardware before it was read.

fields.name: DCEN
fields.description: Timer2 Down Count ENable. Set by firmware only. When this bit is set, Timer2 can be configured (in auto_reload mode) as an up-counter. When this bit is reset, Timer2 can be configured (in auto-reload mode) as a down-counter.

fields.name: RSVD_1
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: R2L
fields.description: Timer2 lower reload byte

fields.name: R2H
fields.description: Timer2 upper reload byte

fields.name: T2L
fields.description: Timer2 timer/counter lower byte

fields.name: T2H
fields.description: Timer2 timer/counter upper byte

fields.name: SMOD
fields.description: Serial MODe: When set to logic 1, the baud rate of the Debug UART is doubled

fields.name: SMOD
fields.description: Serial MODe: When set to logic 1, the baud rate of the Debug UART is doubled

fields.name: RSVD_5
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: SMOD
fields.description: Serial MODe

fields.name: SMOD
fields.description: Serial MODe

fields.name: SMOD
fields.description: Serial MODe

fields.name: CPU_PD
fields.description: Power-down: When set to logic 1, the microcontroller goes in Power-down mode

fields.name: RSVD_0
fields.description: This bit should only ever contain logic 0

Debug UART.name: S0CON
Debug UART.description: Control and status register of the Debug UART containing mode selection bits (SM0, SM1, SM0), 9th data bit for transmit and receive (TB8 and RB8), and serial port interrupt bits (TI and RI).

fields.name: SM0
fields.description: Mode selection bit 0 and 1. Set by firmware only. The Debug UART has 4 modes.

fields.name: SM2
fields.description: Multi-processor communication enable. Set by firmware only.

fields.name: REN
fields.description: Serial reception enable. Set by firmware only. When set to logic 1, enables reception.

fields.name: TB8
fields.description: Transmit data bit. Set by firmware only. In modes 2 and 3, the value of TB8 is transmitted as the 9th data bit. In modes 0 and 1, the TB8 bit is not used.

fields.name: RB8
fields.description: Receive data bit. Set by hardware and by firmware. In modes 2 or 3, the hardware stores the 9th data bit that was received in RB8. In mode 1, the hardware stores the stop bit that was received in RB8. In mode 0, the hardware does not change RB8.

fields.name: TI
fields.description: Transmit interrupt flag. TI must be set to logic 0 by firmware.

fields.name: RI
fields.description: Receive interrupt flag. RI must be set to logic 0 by firmware.

Debug UART.name: S0BUF
Debug UART.description: Debug UART data buffer register. Writing to S0BUF writes to the transmit buffer. Reading from S0BUF reads from the receive buffer.

fields.name: S0BUF
fields.description: Writing to S0BUF writes to the transmit buffer. Reading from S0BUF reads from the receive buffer.

CPU FIFO.name: RWL
CPU FIFO.description: Defines the warning level of the Receive FIFO for the CPU. It implies a FIFO buffer overflow.

fields.name: RWATERLEVEL
fields.description: Overflow threshold of the Receive FIFO to set a warning

CPU FIFO.name: TWL
CPU FIFO.description: Defines the warning level of the Transmit FIFO for the CPU. It implies a FIFO buffer underflow.

fields.name: TWATERLEVEL
fields.description: Underflow threshold of the Transmit FIFO to set a warning

CPU FIFO.name: FIFOFS
CPU FIFO.description: Indicates the number of bytes that the CPU can still load into the FIFO until the Transmit FIFO is full.

fields.name: TRANSMIT_FREESPACE
fields.description: Freespace into the FIFO

CPU FIFO.name: FIFOFF
CPU FIFO.description: Indicates the number of bytes already received and loaded into the Receive FIFO.

fields.name: RECEIVE_FULLNESS
fields.description: Number of bytes received in the FIFO

CPU FIFO.name: SFF
CPU FIFO.description: Used to allow the CPU to monitor the status of the FIFO. The primary purpose is to detect completion of data transfers.

fields.name: FIFO_EN
fields.description: Fifo Enable: Set to logic 1 this bit enables the FIFO manager clock (CPU_CLK). Set to logic 0 the clock remains low.

fields.name: RSVD
fields.description: Reserved.

fields.name: TWLL
fields.description: Transmit WaterlLevelLow: This bit is set to logic 1 when the number of bytes stored into the Transmit FIFO is equal or smaller than the threshold TWaterlevel.

fields.name: TFF
fields.description: Transmit FIFO Full: This is set to logic 1 if the transmit part of the FIFO is full. It is set to logic 0 when a transfer is completed.

fields.name: TFE
fields.description: Transmit FIFO Empty: This bit indicates when the transmit part of the FIFO is empty. It is set to logic 0 when the CPU writes a character in the data register.

fields.name: RWLH
fields.description: Receive WaterLevel High : This bit is set to logic 1 when the number of bytes stored into the Receive FIFO is greater or equal to the threshold RWaterlevel.

fields.name: RFF
fields.description: Receive FIFO Full: This bit is set to logic 1 if the receive part of the FIFO is full. It is set to logic 0 by reading the FDATA register.

fields.name: RFE
fields.description: Receive FIFO Empty: This bit indicates when the receive part of the FIFO is empty. Set to logic 1, when the Receive FIFO is empty. Set to logic 0, when the Receive FIFO contains at least 1 byte.

CPU FIFO.name: FIT
CPU FIFO.description: Contains 6 read-write bits which are logically OR-ed to generate an interrupt going to the CPU.

fields.name: RESET
fields.description: Reset: Set to logic 1, Reset defines that the bits set to logic 1 in the write command are set to logic 0 in the register.

fields.name: RSVD
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: WCOL_IRQ
fields.description: Write COLlision IRQ: This bit is set to logic 1 when the transmitted part of the FIFO is already full (TFF is set to logic 1) and a new character is written by the CPU in the data register.

fields.name: TWLL_IRQ
fields.description: Transmit WaterlLevelLow IRQ: This bit is set to logic 1 when the number of bytes stored into the Transmit FIFO is equal or smaller than the threshold TWaterlevel.

fields.name: TFF_IRQ
fields.description: Transmit FIFO Full IRQ: This is set to logic 1 if the transmitted part of the FIFO is full.

fields.name: RWLH_IRQ
fields.description: Receive WaterLevel High IRQ: This bit is set to logic 1 when the number of bytes stored into the Receive FIFO is greater or equal to the threshold RWaterlevel.

fields.name: ROVR_IRQ
fields.description: Read OVeRrun IRQ: This bit indicates that a read overrun has occured.It occurs when the receiver part of the FIFO is full and a new data transfer is completed. Then the new received data is lost and ROVR_IRQ is set.

fields.name: RFF_IRQ
fields.description: Receive FIFO Full IRQ: This bit is set to logic 1 if the received part of the FIFO is full.

CPU FIFO.name: FITEN
CPU FIFO.description: Enables or disables interrupt requests to the CPU and resets Receive and Transmit FIFO content.

fields.name: TFLUSH
fields.description: When set to 1, resets Transmit FIFO pointer. Must not be set with RFLUSH.

fields.name: RFLUSH
fields.description: When set to 1, resets Receive FIFO pointer. Must not be set with TFLUSH.

fields.name: EN_WCOL_IRQ
fields.description: Enable Write Collision IRQ (requires IE1_5 also set).

fields.name: EN_TWLL_IRQ
fields.description: Enable Transmit WaterLevelLow IRQ (requires IE1_5 also set).

fields.name: EN_TFF_IRQ
fields.description: Enable Transmit FIFO Full IRQ (requires IE1_5 also set).

fields.name: EN_RWLH_IRQ
fields.description: Enable Receive WaterLevel High IRQ (requires IE1_5 also set).

fields.name: EN_ROVR_IRQ
fields.description: Enable Read Overrun IRQ (requires IE1_5 also set).

fields.name: EN_RFF_IRQ
fields.description: Enable Receive FIFO Full IRQ (requires IE1_5 also set).

CPU FIFO.name: FDATA
CPU FIFO.description: Provides transmitted and received data bytes. Write pushes to Transmit FIFO, read pops from Receive FIFO.

fields.name: FDATA
fields.description: Writing writes to transmit buffer. Reading reads from receive buffer.

CPU FIFO.name: FSIZE
CPU FIFO.description: Defines size of Receive FIFO (max 182 bytes). Unused space allocated to Transmit FIFO.

fields.name: RECEIVE_SIZE
fields.description: Size of the Receive FIFO.

fields.name: RSVD_7_6
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: ENABLE
fields.description: SPI enable: When set to logic 1, enables the SPI interface assuming that selif[1:0] are set to 01b.

fields.name: RSVD_4
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: CPHA
fields.description: Clock PHAse: This bit controls the relationship between the data and the clock on SPI transfers. When set to logic 0: Data is always sampled on the first clock edge of SCK. When set to logic 1: Data is always sampled on the second clock edge of SCK.

fields.name: CPOL
fields.description: Clock POLarity: This bit controls the polarity of SCK clock. When set to logic 1, SCK starts from logic 0 else starts from logic 1.

fields.name: IE1
fields.description: Interrupt Enable 1: When set to logic 1, the hardware interrupt generated by TR_FE in SPIstatus register is enabled. The bit IE1_5 of register IE1 (see Table 13 on page 18) has also to be set to logic 1 to enable the corresponding CPU interrupt.

fields.name: IE0
fields.description: Interrupt Enable 0: When set to logic 1, the hardware interrupt generated by RCV_OVR in SPIstatus register is enabled. The bit IE1_5 of register IE1 (see Table 13 on page 18) has also to be set to logic 1 to enable the corresponding CPU interrupt.

SPI.name: SPISTATUS
SPI.description: SPI status register for monitoring SPI interface status and exceptions

fields.name: RSVD
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: TR_FE
fields.description: Transmit FIFO Empty flag

fields.name: RCV_OVR
fields.description: Receive Overrun flag

fields.name: RSVD_1
fields.description: Reserved, must be set to logic 0

fields.name: READY
fields.description: Ready flag set by firmware

fields.name: SET_BIT
fields.description: When set to logic 0 during write operation, the bits set to logic 1 in the write command are written to logic 0 in the register. When set to logic 1 during write operation, the bits set to logic 1 in the write command are written to logic 1 in the register.

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: DISABLE_PREAMB
fields.description: Preamble filter disable. When set to logic 1, this bit disables the preamble filtering.

fields.name: IRQ_RX_OVER_EN
fields.description: FIFO overflow interrupt enable.

fields.name: IRQ_RX_FER_EN
fields.description: Framing error interrupt enable.

fields.name: IRQ_RX_OVER
fields.description: Receive FIFO overflow interrupt.

fields.name: IRQ_RX_FER
fields.description: Framing error interrupt.

HSU.name: HSU_CTR
HSU.description: Controls the configuration of the HSU.

fields.name: HSU_WU_EN
fields.description: HSU wake-up enable.

fields.name: START_FRAME
fields.description: Enables the preamble filter for next frame.

fields.name: TX_STOPBIT
fields.description: Defines the number of stop bit during transmission. tx_stopbit +1.

fields.name: RX_STOPBIT
fields.description: Defines the number of stop bit during reception. rx_stopbit +1.

fields.name: TX_EN
fields.description: Enables the transmission of HSU.

fields.name: RX_EN
fields.description: Enables the reception of the HSU.

fields.name: SOFT_RESET_N
fields.description: HSU Reset. When set to logic 0, disables the clock.

HSU.name: HSU_PRE
HSU.description: Used to configure the baud rate generator prescaler.

fields.name: HSU_PRESCALER
fields.description: HSU prescaler[7:0]. Division factor from 1 to 256.

HSU.name: HSU_CNT
HSU.description: HSU counter register for baud rate generator

fields.name: hsu_counter
fields.description: In conjunction with HSU_PRE, defines the HSU baud rate. Baud rate = f_clk / ((hsu_prescaler +1) * hsu_counter)

fields.name: IE0_7
fields.description: Global interrupt enable When set to logic 1, the interrupts can be enabled. When set to logic 0, all the interrupts are disabled.

fields.name: IE0_6
fields.description: NFC-WI counter interrupt enable When set to logic 1, NFC-WI interrupt is enabled.

fields.name: IE0_5
fields.description: Timer2 interrupt enable When set to logic 1, Timer2 interrupt is enabled.

fields.name: IE0_4
fields.description: Debug UART interrupt enable When set to logic 1, Debug UART interrupt is enabled.

fields.name: IE0_3
fields.description: Timer1 interrupt enable When set to logic 1, Timer1 interrupt is enabled.

fields.name: IE0_2
fields.description: P33_INT1 interrupt enable When set to logic 1, P33_INT1 pin interrupt is enabled.

fields.name: IE0_1
fields.description: Timer0 interrupt enable When set to logic 1, Timer0 interrupt is enabled.

fields.name: IE0_0
fields.description: P32_INT0 interrupt enable When set to logic 1, P32_INT0 pin interrupt is enabled.

fields.name: IP0_7
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: IP0_6
fields.description: When set to logic 1, NFC-WI interrupt is set to high priority.

fields.name: IP0_5
fields.description: When set to logic 1, Timer2 interrupt is set to high priority.

fields.name: IP0_4
fields.description: When set to logic 1, Debug UART interrupt is set to high priority.

fields.name: IP0_3
fields.description: When set to logic 1, Timer1 interrupt is set to high priority.

fields.name: IP0_2
fields.description: When set to logic 1, external P33_INT1 pin is set to high priority.

fields.name: IP0_1
fields.description: When set to logic 1, Timer0 interrupt is set to high priority.

fields.name: IP0_0
fields.description: When set to logic 1, external P32_INT0 pin is set to high priority.

fields.name: IE1_7
fields.description: General purpose IRQ interrupt enable . When set to logic 1, enables interrupt function of P34, P35, P50_SCL and P71 according to their respective enable and level control bits. See Table 19 on page 20, Table 137 on page 95 and Table 143 on page 97.

fields.name: RSVD
fields.description: Reserved. This bit must be set to logic 0

fields.name: IE1_5
fields.description: FIFO, SPI and HSU interrupt enable . When set to logic 1, enables FIFO interrupts, SPI interrupts, HSU interrupt. In HSU mode, the interrupt is when NSS is at logic 0. For the FIFO interrupts, see Table 112 on page 76. For the SPI interrupts, see Table 122 on page 81.

fields.name: IE1_4
fields.description: I2C interrupt enable . When set to logic 1, enables I^2 C interrupt. See Table 77 on page 54.

fields.name: IE1_3
fields.description: CIU interrupt 0 enable . When set to logic 1, enables CIU interrupt 0: CIU_IRQ_0. See Table 190 on page 151.

fields.name: IE1_2
fields.description: CIU interrupt 1 enable. When set to logic 1, enables the CIU interrupt 1: CIU_IRQ_1. See Table 190 on page 151.

fields.name: RSVD_1
fields.description: Reserved. This bit must be set to logic 0.

fields.name: IE1_0
fields.description: LDO overcurrent interrupt enable. When set to logic 1, enables the LDO overcurrent detection interrupt. See Table 127 on page 88.

fields.name: IP1_7
fields.description: When set to logic 1, General Purpose IRQ interrupt is set to high priority.

fields.name: RSVD_6
fields.description: Reserved. This bit must be set to logic 0.

fields.name: IP1_5
fields.description: When set to logic 1, combined SPI, FIFO and HSU interrupt is set to high priority.

fields.name: IP1_4
fields.description: When set to logic 1, I^2 C interrupt is set to high priority.

fields.name: IP1_3
fields.description: When set to logic 1, CIU interrupt 0 is set to high priority.

fields.name: IP1_2
fields.description: When set to logic 1, CIU interrupt 1 is set to high priority.

fields.name: RSVD_1
fields.description: Reserved. This bit must be set to logic 0.

fields.name: IP1_0
fields.description: When set to logic 1, interrupt number 7 is set to high priority.

fields.name: RSVD
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: P3_5
fields.description: Writing to P3[5] writes the corresponding value to P35 pin according to the configuration mode defined by P3CFGA[5] and P3CFGB[5]. Reading from P3[5] reads the state of P35 pin.

fields.name: P3_4
fields.description: When P34 alternate function SIC_CLK is not used, writing to P3[4] writes the corresponding value to P34 pin according to the configuration mode defined by P3CFGA[4] and P3CFGB[4]. Reading from P3[4] reads the state of P34 pin.

fields.name: P3_3
fields.description: Writing to P3[3] writes the corresponding value to P33_INT1 pin according to the configuration mode defined by P3CFGA[3] and P3CFGB[3]. Reading from P3[3] reads the state of P33_INT1 pin.

fields.name: P3_2
fields.description: Writing to P3[2] writes the corresponding value to P32_INT0 pin according to the configuration mode defined by P3CFGA[2] and P3CFGB[2]. Reading from P3[2] reads the state of P32_INT0 pin.

fields.name: P3_1
fields.description: When the P31 pin alternate function UART_TX is not used, writing to P3[1] writes the corresponding value to P31 pin according to the configuration mode defined by P3CFGA[1] and P3CFGB[1]. Reading from P3[1] reads the state of P31 pin.

fields.name: P3_0
fields.description: When the P30 pin alternate function UART_RX is not used, writing to P3[0] writes the corresponding value to P30 pin according to the configuration mode defined by P3CFGA[0] and P3CFGB[0]. Reading from P3[0] reads the state of P30 pin.

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: P7CFGA_2
fields.description: Configures P72 pin mode with P7CFGB[2]

fields.description: Configures P71 pin mode with P7CFGB[1]

fields.name: P7CFGA_0
fields.description: Configures P70_IRQ pin mode with P7CFGB[0]

fields.description: Configures P71 pin mode with P7CFGA[1]

fields.name: P7CFGB_0
fields.description: Configures P70_IRQ pin mode with P7CFGA[0]

fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: P7_2
fields.description: Out of SPI mode: Writing to P7[2] writes the corresponding value to the P72 pin according to the configuration mode defined by P7CFGA[2] and P7CFGB[2]. Reading from P7[2] reads the state of P72 pin.

fields.name: P7_1
fields.description: Out of SPI mode: Writing to P7[1] writes the corresponding value to the P71 pin according to the configuration mode defined by P7CFGA[1] and P7CFGB[1]. Reading from P7[1] reads the state of P71 pin.

fields.name: P7_0
fields.description: Writing to P7[0] writes the corresponding value to the P70_IRQ pin according to the configuration mode defined by P7CFGA[0] and P7CFGB[0]. Reading from P7[0] reads the state of P70_IRQ pin.

fields.description: Reserved

fields.description: P3CFGA[5]

fields.description: P3CFGA[4]

fields.description: P3CFGA[3]

fields.description: P3CFGA[1]

fields.description: P3CFGA[0]

fields.description: In conjuction with P3CFGA[5], it configures the functional mode of P35.

CIU.name: CIU_MODE
CIU.description: Defines general modes for transmitting and receiving.

fields.name: MSBFirst
fields.description: Set to logic 1, the CRC co-processor calculates the CRC with MSB first. The bit order in the registers CRCResultMSB and the CIU_CRCResultLSB is reversed. Note: During RF communication this bit is ignored.

fields.name: DetectSync
fields.description: If set to logic 1, the CIU waits for the F0h byte before the receiver is activated and F0h byte is added as a Sync-byte for transmission. This bit is only valid for 106 kbit/s during NFCIP-1 data exchange protocol. In all other modes it shall be set to logic 0.

fields.name: TXWaitRF
fields.description: Set to logic 1 the transmitter in Reader/Writer or Initiator mode for NFCIP-1 can only be started, if an own RF field is generated (i.e. Tx1RFEn and/or Tx2RFen is set to logic 1).

fields.name: RxWaitRF
fields.description: Set to logic 1, the counter for RxWait starts only, if an external RF field is detected in Target mode for NFCIP-1 or in Card Operating mode.

fields.name: PolSigin
fields.description: PolSigin defines the polarity of the SIGIN pin. Set to logic 1, the polarity of SIGIN pin is active high. Set to logic 0 the polarity of SIGIN pin is active low. Note: The internal envelope signal is coded active low. Note: Changing this bit will generate a SiginActIrq event.

fields.name: ModeDetOff
fields.description: Set to logic 1, the internal Data Mode Detector is switched off. Note: The Data Mode Detector is only active during the AutoColl command.

fields.name: CRCPreset
fields.description: Defines the preset value for the CRC co-processor for the CalCRC command.

CIU.name: CIU_TXMODE
CIU.description: Defines the transmission data rate and framing during transmission.

fields.name: TxCRCEn
fields.description: Set to logic 1, this bit enables the CRC generation during data transmission. Note: This bit shall only set to logic 0 at 106 kbit/s.

fields.name: TxSpeed
fields.description: Defines bit rate while data transmission.

fields.name: InvMod
fields.description: Set to logic 1, the modulation for transmitting data is inverted.

fields.name: TxMix
fields.description: Set to logic 1, the signal at SIGIN is mixed with the internal coder. See Section 8.6.12 'Serial data switch' on page 121.

fields.name: TxFraming
fields.description: Defines the framing used for data transmission.

enum_values.0: ISO/IEC 14443A/MIFARE and Passive Communication mode 106 kbit/s
enum_values.2: FeliCa and Passive Communication mode at 212 kbit/s and 424 kbit/s

CIU.name: CIU_RXMODE
CIU.description: Defines the reception data rate and framing during receiving.

fields.name: RXCRCEN
fields.description: Set to logic 1, this bit enables the CRC calculation during reception. The CRC bytes will not be written within the CIU FIFO. Note: This bit shall only set to logic 0 at 106 kbit/s.

fields.name: RXSPEED
fields.description: Defines the bit rate while data receiving. 000=106 kbit/s, 001=212 kbit/s, 010=424 kbit/s, 011=848 kbit/s, 100=1696 kbit/s, 101=3392 kbit/s, 110-111=Reserved.

fields.name: RXNOERR
fields.description: If set to logic 1, a not valid received data stream (less than 4 bits received) will be ignored. The receiver will remain active. Set to logic 0, the receiver is deactivated after receiving a data frame.

fields.name: RXMULTIPLE
fields.description: Set to logic 1, it is possible to receive more than one data frame. This bit is only valid for 212 and 424 kbit/s to handle the Polling command.

fields.name: RXFRAMING
fields.description: Defines the expected framing for data reception. 00=ISO/IEC 14443A/MIFARE and Passive Communication mode 106 kbit/s, 01=Active communication mode, 10=FeliCa and Passive Communication mode at 212 kbit/s and 424 kbit/s, 11=ISO/IEC 14443B.

enum_values.0b00: ISO/IEC 14443A/MIFARE and Passive Communication mode 106 kbit/s
enum_values.0b10: FeliCa and Passive Communication mode at 212 kbit/s and 424 kbit/s

CIU.name: CIU_TXCONTROL
CIU.description: Controls the logical behavior of the antenna driver pins TX1 and TX2.

fields.name: INVTX2RFON
fields.description: Set to logic1 and Tx2RFEn set to logic 1, TX2 output signal is inverted.

fields.name: INVTX1RFON
fields.description: Set to logic1 and Tx1RFEn set to logic 1, TX1 output signal is inverted.

fields.name: INVTX2RFOFF
fields.description: Set to logic1 and Tx2RFEn set to logic 0, TX2 output signal is inverted.

fields.name: INVTX1RFOFF
fields.description: Set to logic1 and Tx1RFEn set to logic 0, TX1 output signal is inverted.

fields.name: TX2CW
fields.description: Set to logic 1, the output signal on pin TX2 will deliver continuously the un-modulated 13.56 MHz energy carrier. Set to logic 0, Tx2CW is enabled to modulate of the 13.56 MHz energy carrier.

fields.name: CHECKRF
fields.description: Set to logic 1, Tx2RFEn and Tx1RFEn can not be set if an external RF field is detected. Only valid when using in combination with Tx2RFAutoEn and TX1RFAutoEn bits in CIU_TxAuto register.

fields.name: TX2RFEN
fields.description: Set to logic 1, the output signal on pin TX2 will deliver the 13.56 MHz energy carrier modulated by the transmission data.

fields.name: TX1RFEN
fields.description: Set to logic 1, the output signal on pin TX1 will deliver the 13.56 MHz energy carrier modulated by the transmission data.

CIU.name: CIU_TXAUTO
CIU.description: Controls the setting of the antenna driver.

fields.name: AUTORFOFF
fields.description: Set to logic 1, own RF field is switched off after the last data bit has been transmitted as defined in the NFCIP-1 standard.

fields.name: FORCE100ASK
fields.description: Set to logic 1, Force100ASK forces a 100% ASK modulation independent of the setting in CIU_ModGsP register.

fields.name: AUTOWAKEUP
fields.description: Set to logic 1, the PN532 in CIU Power-down mode can be woken up by the RF level detector.

fields.name: CAON
fields.description: Set to logic 1, the collision avoidance is activated and internally the value n is set in accordance to the ISO/IEC 18092 / ECMA340 NFCIP-1 standards.

fields.name: INITIALRFON
fields.description: Set to logic 1, the initial RF collision avoidance is performed and the bit InitialRFOn is set to logic 0 automatically, if the RF is switched ON. Note: The driver(s) which should be switched on, have to enabled by

fields.name: TX2RFAUTOEN
fields.description: Set to logic 1, RF is switched on at TX2 (i.e. Tx2RFEn is set to logic 1) after the external RF field is switched off according to the time TADT. If the InitialRFOn and Tx2RFAutoEn bits are set to logic 1, RF is switched on at TX2 if no external RF field is detected during the time TIDT. Note: The times TADT and TIDT are in accordance to the ISO/IEC 18092 /

fields.name: TX1RFAUTOEN
fields.description: Set to logic 1, RF is switched on at TX1(i.e. Tx1RFEn is set to logic 1) after the external RF field is switched off according to the time TADT. If the InitialRFOn and Tx1RFAutoEn bits are set to logic 1, RF is switched on at TX1 if no external RF field is detected during the time TIDT. Note: The times TADT and TIDT are in accordance to the ISO/IEC 18092 / ECMA340 NFCIP-1 standards.

fields.name: RSVD_4
fields.description: Reserved

CIU.name: CIU_TX_SEL
CIU.description: Selects the sources for the analogue transmitter part

fields.name: LOAD_MOD_SEL
fields.description: Selects the signal to be output on LOADMOD

enum_values.0b01: Modulation signal (envelope) from the internal coder
enum_values.0b11: Test signal defined by LoadModtest in register CIU_TestSel1

fields.name: DRIVER_SEL
fields.description: Selects the signals to be output on Tx1 and Tx2

enum_values.0b01: Modulation signal (envelope) from the internal coder

fields.name: SIG_OUT_SEL
fields.description: Select the signal to be output on SIGOUT

enum_values.0b0011: Test bus signal as defined by TestBusBitSel in CIU_TestSel1
enum_values.0b0100: Modulation signal (envelope) from the internal coder
enum_values.0b0101: Serial data stream to be transmitted
enum_values.0b0110: Output signal of the receiver circuit (card modulation signal regenerated and delayed)

CIU.name: CIU_RX_SEL
CIU.description: Selects internal receiver settings

fields.name: UART_SEL
fields.description: Selects the input of the digital part (CL UART) of the CIU

enum_values.0b10: Modulation signal from the internal analog part
enum_values.0b11: Modulation signal from SIGIN pin. Only valid for transfer speeds above 424 kbit/s

fields.name: RX_WAIT
fields.description: After data transmission, the activation of the receiver is delayed for RxWait bit-clocks. During this 'frame guard time' any signal at pin Rx is ignored.

CIU.name: CIU_RX_THRESHOLD
CIU.description: Selects thresholds for the bit decoder.

fields.name: MinLevel
fields.description: Defines the minimum signal strength at the decoder input that shall be accepted. If the signal strength is below this level, it is not evaluated.

fields.name: RSVD
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: Collevel
fields.description: Defines the minimum signal strength at the decoder input that has to be reached by the weaker half-bit of the Manchester-coded signal to generate a bit-collision relatively to the amplitude of the stronger half-bit.

CIU.name: CIU_DEMOD
CIU.description: Defines demodulator settings.

fields.name: AddIQ
fields.description: Defines the use of I and Q channel during reception. Note: FixIQ has to be set to logic 0 to enable the following settings. ValueDescription 00Select the stronger channel 01Select the stronger and freeze the selected during communication 10Combines the I and Q channel 11RFU

enum_values.0b01: Select the stronger and freeze the selected during communication
enum_values.0b10: Combines the I and Q channel

fields.name: FixIQ
fields.description: If set to logic 1 and AddIQ[0] is set to logic 0, the reception is fixed to I channel. If set to logic 1 and AddIQ[0] is set to logic 1, the reception is fixed to Q channel.

fields.name: RSVD
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: TauRcv
fields.description: Changes time-constant of internal PLL during data receiving. Note: If set to 00h, the PLL is frozen during data receiving.

fields.name: TauSync
fields.description: Changes time-constant of internal PLL during burst (out of data reception)

CIU.name: CIU_FEL_NFC1
CIU.description: Defines the length of the valid range for the received frame

CIU.name: CIU_FELNFC1
CIU.description: Defines the length of the FeliCa Sync bytes and the minimum length of the received frame.

fields.name: FelSyncLen
fields.description: Defines the length of the Sync bytes. Value 00 B2 4D 01 00 B2 4D 10 00 00 B2 4D 11 00 00 00 B2 4D

fields.name: DataLenMin
fields.description: These bits define the minimum length of the accepted frame length. DataLenMin 4 × DataPacketLenght ≤ This parameter is ignored at 106 kbit/s if the DetectSync bit in CIU_Mode register is set to logic 0. If a received frame is shorter as the defined DataLenMin value, the frame will be ignored.

CIU.name: CIU_FEL_NFC2
CIU.description: FEL NFC2 control register

fields.name: WAIT_FOR_SELECTED
fields.description: WaitForSelected

fields.name: SHORT_TIME_SLOT
fields.description: ShortTimeSlot

fields.name: DATA_LEN_MAX
fields.description: DataLenMax[5:0]

CIU.name: CIU_FELNFC2
CIU.description: Defines the maximum length of the received frame.

fields.name: WaitForSelected
fields.description: Set to logic 1, the AutoColl command is automatically ended only when: 1. A valid command has been received after performing a valid Select procedure according to ISO/IEC 14443A. 2. A valid command has been received after performing a valid Polling procedure according to the FeliCa specification. Note: If this bit is set, no Active Communication is possible. Note: Setting this bit reduces the 80C51 interaction in case of a communication to another device in the same RF field during Passive Communication mode.

fields.name: ShortTimeSlot
fields.description: Defines the time slot length for Active Communication mode at 424 kbit/s. Set to logic 1 a short time slot is used (half of the timeslot at 212 kbit/s). Set to logic 0 a long timeslot is used (equal to the timeslot for 212 kbit/s).

fields.name: DataLenMax
fields.description: These bits define the maximum length of the accepted frame length: DataLenMax 4 × DataPacketLenght ≥ Note: If set to logic 0 the maximum data length is 256 bytes. This parameter is ignored at 106 kbit/s if the bit DetectSync in register CIU_Mode is set to logic 0. If a received frame is larger as the defined DataLenMax value, the frame will be ignored.

CIU.name: CIU_MIF_NFC
CIU.description: Defines ISO/IEC 14443A/MIFARE/NFC specific settings in target or card operating mode.

fields.name: SENS_MILLER
fields.description: This bit defines the sensitivity of the Miller decoder.

fields.name: TAU_MILLER
fields.description: This bit defines the time constant of the Miller decoder.

fields.name: MF_HALTED
fields.description: Set to logic 1, this bit indicates that the CIU is set to HALT mode in Card Operating mode at 106 kbit/s.

fields.name: TX_WAIT
fields.description: In combination with TxBitPhase[6:0] in CIU_TxBitPhase register, defines the additional response time for the target at 106 kbit/s.

CIU.name: CIU_MANUAL_RCV
CIU.description: Allows manual fine tuning of the internal receiver. For standard application it is not recommended to change this register settings.

fields.name: RSVD
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: FAST_FILT_MF_SO
fields.description: If this bit is set to logic 1, the internal filter for the Miller-Delay circuit is set to Fast-Mode.

fields.name: DELAY_MF_SO
fields.description: If this bit is set to logic 1, when SigoutSel=1100b (register 6306h), the Signal at SIGOUT-pin is delayed.

fields.name: PARITY_DISABLE
fields.description: If this bit is set to logic 1, the generation of the Parity bit for transmission and the parity check for receiving is switched off.

fields.name: LARGE_BW_PLL
fields.description: Set to logic 1, the bandwidth of the internal PLL for clock recovery is extended.

fields.name: MANUAL_HPCF
fields.description: Set to logic 0, the HPCF[1:0] bits are ignored and the HPCF[1:0] settings are adapted automatically to the receiving mode.

fields.name: HPCF
fields.description: Selects the High Pass Corner Frequency (HPCF) of the filter in the internal receiver chain.

enum_values.0: For signals with frequency spectrum down to 106 kHz
enum_values.1: For signals with frequency spectrum down to 212 kHz
enum_values.2: For signals with frequency spectrum down to 424 kHz
enum_values.3: For signals with frequency spectrum down to 848 kHz

CIU.name: CIU_TYPEB
CIU.description: Selects the specific settings for the ISO/IEC 14443B

fields.name: RX_SOF_REQ
fields.description: If set to 1, SOF is required. Datastream without SOF ignored. If 0, SOF optional and removed.

fields.name: RX_EOF_REQ
fields.description: If set to 1, EOF required. Without EOF sets ProtocollErr. If 0, EOF optional and removed.

fields.name: RSV
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: EOF_SOF_WIDTH
fields.description: 1: SOF/EOF max length. 0: min length per ISO/IEC 14443B

fields.name: NO_TX_SOF
fields.description: If set to 1, generation of SOF is suppressed

fields.name: NO_TX_EOF
fields.description: If set to 1, generation of EOF is suppressed

fields.name: TX_EGT
fields.description: Defines EGT length per ISO/IEC 14443B

CIU.name: CIU_CRCRESULTMSB
CIU.description: Shows the actual MSB values of the CRC calculation.

fields.name: CRCRESULTMSB
fields.description: Actual value of the most significant byte of the CRC calculation. Valid only if CRCReady bit in CIU_Status1 register is set to logic 1.

CIU.name: CIU_CRCRESULTLSB
CIU.description: Shows the actual LSB values of the CRC calculation.

fields.name: CRCRESULTLSB
fields.description: Actual value of the least significant byte of the CRC calculation. Valid only if CRCReady bit in CIU_Status1 register is set to logic 1.

CIU.name: CIU_GSNOFF
CIU.description: Selects the conductance for the N-driver of the antenna driver pins TX1 and TX2 when there is no RF generated by the PN532.

fields.name: CWGSNOFF
fields.description: Conductance of the output N-driver during the time of no modulation and when there is no RF generated by the PN532.

fields.name: MODGSNOFF
fields.description: Conductance of the output N-driver for the time of modulation and when there is no RF generated by the PN532.

CIU.name: CIU_MODWIDTH
CIU.description: Controls the setting of the modulation width.

fields.name: MODWIDTH
fields.description: Defines the width of the Miller modulation as initiator in Active and Passive Communication mode as multiples of the carrier frequency.

CIU.name: CIU_TXBITPHASE
CIU.description: Adjust the bit phase at 106 kbit/s during transmission.

fields.name: RCVCLKCHANGE
fields.description: Set to logic 1, the demodulator's clock is derived from the external RF field.

fields.name: TXBITPHASE
fields.description: Define a delay to adjust the bit synchronization during Passive Communication mode at 106 kbit/s.

CIU.name: CIU_RFCFG
CIU.description: Configures the receiver gain and RF level detector sensitivity.

fields.name: RFLEVEL_AMP
fields.description: Set to logic 1, this bit activates the RF level detector's amplifier.

fields.name: RXGAIN
fields.description: Receiver signal voltage gain factor.

fields.name: RFLEVEL
fields.description: Sensitivity of the RF level detector.

CIU.name: CIU_GSNON
CIU.description: Selects the conductance for the N-driver of the antenna driver pins TX1 and TX2 when generating RF.

fields.name: CWGSNON
fields.description: Conductance of the output N-driver during times of no modulation when generating RF field.

fields.name: MODGSNON
fields.description: Conductance of the output N-driver for the time of modulation when generating RF field.

CIU.name: CIU_CWGSP
CIU.description: Defines the conductance of the P-driver.

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: Reserved.

fields.name: CWGSP
fields.description: Conductance of the output P-driver during times of no modulation.

CIU.name: CIU_MOD_GSP
CIU.description: Defines the driver P-output conductance for the time of modulation.

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: Reserved.

fields.name: MOD_GSP
fields.description: The value of this register defines the conductance of the output P-driver for the time of modulation.

CIU.name: CIU_TMODE
CIU.description: Defines settings for the internal timer.

fields.name: TAUTO
fields.description: Set to logic 1, the timer starts automatically at the end of the transmission in all communication modes at all speed, or when InitialRFOn (in CIU_TxAuto) is set to logic 1 and the external RF field is switched on.

fields.name: TGATED
fields.description: The internal timer is running in gated mode.

fields.name: TAUTO_RESTART
fields.description: Set to logic 1 the timer automatically restart its count-down from TReloadValue defined within when reaches zero.

fields.name: TPRESCALER_HI
fields.description: Defines higher 4 bits for the TPrescaler.

CIU.name: CIU_TPRESCALER
CIU.description: Define the LSB of the Timer-Prescaler.

fields.name: TPRESCALER_LO
fields.description: Defines lower 8 bits for TPrescaler.

CIU.name: CIU_TRELOADVAL_HI
CIU.description: Defines the MSB of the 16-bit long timer reload value.

fields.name: TRELOADVAL_HI
fields.description: Defines the higher 8 bits for the TReloadValue.

CIU.name: CIU_TRELOADVAL_LO
CIU.description: Defines the LSB of the 16 bit long timer reload value.

fields.name: TRELOADVAL_LO
fields.description: Defines the lower 8 bits for the TReloadValue.

CIU.description: Timer counter value high register

CIU.name: CIU_AUTOTEST
CIU.description: Controls the digital self-test.

fields.name: RSV

fields.name: AMPRCV

fields.description: Enables the digital Self Test. The self-test can be started by the Selftest command in the CIU_Command register. The self-test is enabled by 1001. Note: For default operation the self-test has to be disabled (0000).

CIU.name: CIU_COMMAND
CIU.description: Starts and stops the command execution

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: RcvOff
fields.description: Set to logic 1, the analog part of the receiver is switched off.

fields.name: Power_down
fields.description: Set to logic 1, the CIU Power-down mode is entered. Set to logic 0, the PN532 starts the wake up procedure.

fields.name: Command
fields.description: Activates a command according the Command Code. Reading this register shows, which command is actually executed.

CIU.name: CIU_COMMIEN
CIU.description: Control bits to enable and disable the passing of interrupt requests

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: TxIEn
fields.description: When set to logic 1, allows the transmitter interrupt request (indicated by bit TxIRq) to be propagated to CIU_IRQ_1.

fields.name: RxIEn
fields.description: When set to logic 1, allows the receiver interrupt request (indicated by bit RxIRq) to be propagated to CIU_IRQ_1.

fields.name: IdleIEn
fields.description: When set to logic 1, allows the idle interrupt request (indicated by bit IdleIRq) to be propagated to CIU_IRQ_0.

fields.name: HiAlertIEn
fields.description: When set to logic 1, allows the high alert interrupt request (indicated by bit HiAlertIRq) to be propagated to CIU_IRQ_1.

fields.name: LoAlertIEn
fields.description: When set to logic 1, allows the low alert interrupt request (indicated by bit LoAlertIRq) to be propagated to CIU_IRQ_1.

fields.name: ErrIEn
fields.description: When set to logic 1, allows the error interrupt request (indicated by bit ErrIRq) to be propagated to CIU_IRQ_0.

fields.name: TimerIEn
fields.description: When set to logic 1, allows the timer interrupt request (indicated by bit TimerIRq) to be propagated to CIU_IRQ_0.

CIU.name: CIU_DIVIEN
CIU.description: Controls bits to enable and disable the passing of interrupt requests

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: SiginActIEn
fields.description: Allows the SIGIN active interrupt request to be propagated to CIU_IRQ_0.

fields.name: ModeIEn
fields.description: When set to logic 1, allows the mode interrupt request (indicated by bit ModeIRq) to be propagated to CIU_IRQ_0.

fields.name: CRCIEn
fields.description: When set to logic 1, allows the CRC interrupt request (indicated by bit CRCIRq) to be propagated to CIU_IRQ_0.

fields.name: RfOnIEn
fields.description: When set to logic 1, allows the RF field on interrupt request (indicated by bit RfOnIRq) to be propagated to CIU_IRQ_0.

fields.name: RfOffIEn
fields.description: When set to logic 1, allows the RF field off interrupt request (indicated by bit RfOffIRq) to be propagated to CIU_IRQ_0.

CIU.name: CIU_COMMIRQ
CIU.description: Contains common CIU interrupt request flags

fields.name: Set1
fields.description: When set to logic 0 during write operation, the bit set to logic 1 in the write command are written to logic 0 in the register. When set to logic 1 during write operation, the bit set to logic 1 in the write command are written to logic 1 in the register.

fields.name: TxIRq
fields.description: Set to logic 1, immediately after the last bit of the transmitted data was sent out.

fields.name: RxIRq
fields.description: Set to logic 1 when the receiver detects the end of a valid datastream.

fields.name: IdleIrq
fields.description: Set to logic 1, when a command terminates by itself e.g. when the CIU_Command register changes its value from any command to the Idle command.

fields.name: HiAlertIRq
fields.description: Set to logic 1, when HiAlert bit in CIU_Status1 register is set to logic 1. In opposition to HiAlert, HiAlertIRq stores this event and can only be reset by Set1 bit.

fields.name: LoAlertIRq
fields.description: Set to logic 1, when LoAlert bit in CIU_Status1 register is set. In opposition to LoAlert, LoAlertIRq stores this event and can only be reset by Set1 bit.

fields.name: ErrIRq
fields.description: Set to logic 1, if any error flag in the CIU_Error register is set.

fields.name: TimerIRq
fields.description: Set to logic 1, when the timer decrements the TimerValue register to zero.

CIU.name: CIU_DIV_IRQ
CIU.description: Contains miscellaneous interrupt request flags. These bits are latched.

fields.name: SET2
fields.description: When set to logic level 0 during write operation, the bit set to logic 1 in the write command are written to logic 0 in the register. When set to logic level 1 during write operation, the bit set to logic 1 in the write command are written to logic 1 in the register.

fields.name: SIGIN_ACT_IRQ
fields.description: Set to logic 1, when SIGIN is active. See Section 8.6.13 'NFC-WI/S^2 C interface support' on page 123. This interrupt is set to logic 1 when either a rising or falling edge is detected on SIGIN.

fields.name: MODE_IRQ
fields.description: Set to logic level 1, when the mode has been detected by the Data Mode Detector. Note: The Data Mode Detector can only be activated by the AutoColl command and is terminated automatically having the detected the communication mode. Note: The Data Mode Detector is automatically restarted after each RF reset.

fields.name: CRC_IRQ
fields.description: Set to logic level 1, when the CRC command is active and all data are processed.

fields.name: RF_ON_IRQ
fields.description: Set to logic level 1, when an external RF field is detected.

fields.name: RF_OFF_IRQ
fields.description: Set to logic level 1, when an present external RF field is switched off.

fields.name: RSVD_6_5
fields.description: Reserved

CIU.name: CIU_ERROR
CIU.description: Error flags showing the error status of the last command executed.

fields.name: WR_ERR
fields.description: Set to logic 1, when data is written into the FIFO by the 80C51 during the AutoColl command or MFAuthent command or if data is written into the FIFO by the 80C51 during the time between sending the last bit on the RF interface and receiving the last bit on the RF interface.

fields.name: TEMP_ERR
fields.description: Set to logic 1, if the internal temperature sensor detects overheating. In this case the antenna drivers are switched off automatically.

fields.name: RF_ERR
fields.description: Set to logic 1, if in active communication mode the counterpart does not switch on the RF field in time as defined in NFCIP-1 standard.

fields.name: BUFFER_OVFL
fields.description: Set to logic 1, if the 80C51 or if the internal state machine (e.g. receiver) tries to write data into the FIFO buffer although the FIFO buffer is already full.

fields.name: COLL_ERR
fields.description: Set to logic 1, if a bit-collision is detected. It is set to logic 0 automatically at receiver start phase.

fields.name: CRC_ERR
fields.description: Set to logic 1, if RxCRCEn in CIU_RxMode register is set to logic 1 and the CRC calculation fails.

fields.name: PARITY_ERR
fields.description: Set to logic 1, if the parity check has failed. It is set to logic 0 automatically at receiver start-up phase.

fields.name: PROTOCOL_ERR
fields.description: Set to logic 1, if one out of the following cases occurs: SOF incorrect, byte length violation, etc.

I2C.name: I2CCON
I2C.description: I^2 C control register. CPU can read/write this 8-bit SFR. SI set by hardware on interrupt, STO cleared on STOP or when ENS1=0.

fields.name: CR
fields.description: Serial clock frequency selection in Master mode. Together with CR[1:0], this bit determines the clock rate (serial clock frequency) when the I^2 C interface is in a Master mode.

fields.name: ENS1
fields.description: Serial IO enable. When ENS1 bit is to logic 0, SDA and P50_SCL are in high impedance. The state of SDA and P50_SCL is ignored, the I^2 C interface is in the 'not addressed' Slave state, and the STO bit in I^2 CCON is forced to logic 0. No other bits are affected.

fields.name: STA
fields.description: START control. When the STA bit is set to logic 1 to enter Master mode, the I^2 C interface hardware checks the status of the I^2 C bus and generates a START condition if the bus is free.

fields.name: STO
fields.description: STOP control. When the STO bit is set to logic 1, while the I^2 C interface is in Master mode, a STOP condition is transmitted to the I^2 C bus.

fields.name: SI
fields.description: Serial interrupt flag. When SI is set to logic 1, then if the serial interrupt from the I^2 C interface port is enabled, the CPU will receive an interrupt.

fields.name: AA
fields.description: Assert Acknowledge flag. If AA is set to logic 1, an acknowledge (low level to SDA) will be returned during the acknowledge clock pulse on the P50_SCL line.

I2C.name: I2CSTA
I2C.description: I^2C status register (SFR: address D9h). Read-only. Contains status code. Reset initializes to F8h.

fields.name: ST
fields.description: Encoded status bit for the different functional mode. Several Status codes are returned in a certain mode (Master Transmitter, Master Receiver, Slave Transmitter, Slave Receiver) plus some miscellaneous status codes.

I2C.name: I2C_DAT
I2C.description: I^2C data register containing byte to transmit or received byte

fields.name: I2C_DAT
fields.description: I2C data. Eight bits to be transmitted or just received

fields.name: SA
fields.description: Slave address. 7-bit address recognized on I^2C bus

fields.name: GC
